doc: 20-gene set for PRD-guide-design v0.2 §4.3 hard-gate validation against CRISPOR. Coordinates are NCBI RefSeq GRCh38 (1-based inclusive on the reference, padded to whole-gene window matching the BRCA1 entry already in _RESOLVE_GENE_TO_LOCUS). Stratification rationale below.
crispor_cli_invocation: python3 crispor.py --pam=NGG --maxOcc=60000 --mm=4 --guideLen=20 hg38 input.fa guides.tsv -o offtargets.tsv

tier_a_canonical_validation.doc: High-citation canonical CRISPR validation loci. These are the genes any new guide-design tool is expected to reproduce CRISPOR top-N agreement on; they appear in Doench 2014 / 2016 / Hsu 2013 / Listgarten 2018 figures.

tier_b_listgarten_2018_guide_seq_interse.doc: The 4 genes flagged by state/spike/composite_scoring/phase0_audit.json as the Doench-2016 ∩ Listgarten-2018-GUIDE-seq paired-row intersection. These are the only entries with both on-target activity AND experimental off-target (GUIDE-seq) measurements available; bonus value for off-target gate. Per Phase 0 audit `doench_2016_x_listgarten_2018_guide_seq` = 4 distinct sgRNAs, which map back to a small list of host genes — we pin VEGFA/EMX1/FANCF/RNF2 (the canonical Listgarten 2018 4-guide validation set; intersects tier_a).
tier_b_listgarten_2018_guide_seq_interse.note: Set-overlap with tier_a is intentional and noted in the per-gene `tiers` array below.

tier_c_screen_essential_or_disease_genes.doc: Brunello-screen / disease-relevant loci representative of typical bench-biologist queries. Spread across chromosomes to surface any per-chromosome resolver bug.

genes.note: DNA-damage repair; canonical exon-2 guides in Doench 2016. Pre-existing entry; unchanged.
DNA-damage repair sister gene to BRCA1.
Empty Spiracles Homeobox 1; the canonical 'EMX1 site' is a single-locus gene (~1 kbp). Listgarten 2018 paired.
Fanconi Anemia complementation group F; classic Doench RS1/RS2 training-set locus. Listgarten 2018 paired.
Ring Finger Protein 2; Doench/Listgarten paired locus.
VEGFA site 1/2/3 are the canonical Tsai 2015 GUIDE-seq guides; Listgarten 2018 paired.
Hemoglobin Beta; sickle-cell editing target (CIRCLE-seq / GUIDE-seq class).
Tumor suppressor; Brunello-screened heavily.
Ataxia-telangiectasia mutated; large gene, exercises locus-size scaling.
Compact oncogene; Brunello-screened.
HIV co-receptor; classic safe-harbor / clinical editing target.
Doench 2016 STable 18 CD33_OffTargetdata source — derived the Hsu/Doench mismatch matrix used in CFD.
GPCR; Tsai 2015 / Listgarten 2018 referenced.
Compact oncogene.
DNA-demethylase; large gene.
Hematopoiesis transcription factor; large locus.
Maintenance DNA methyltransferase.
Doench 2016 STable 14 source screen locus; X-chromosome smoke.
PPP1R12C intron / safe-harbor locus widely used for transgene knock-in.
Cystic-fibrosis target; large gene with high-citation editing literature.

limitations: Coordinates pinned from NCBI RefSeq GRCh38.p14 / Annotation Release 110 entries via public NCBI Gene records; not programmatically resolved at fixture-author time. CRISPOR's default RefSeq pin is 109 — boundary cases on a small number of genes may differ. Document any boundary mismatches in DELTAS.md.
AAVS1 is not a HGNC gene symbol; it's a locus name (PPP1R12C intron 1). Keeping the 'AAVS1' alias because CRISPOR recognises it via its bioGuides table and bench biologists reach for it by name.

CRISPOR resolves gene symbols via its own internal annotation; bionpu Tier 1 resolver hardcodes coords. The two paths can disagree on which transcript variant is the primary; this fixture uses NCBI primary-RefSeq spans.
